---
title: 'Class Assignment/Paper: INTERPRETATION OF 2 SAMUEL 7.1-17'
date: '2024-12-02T00:13:00.0090000+00:00'
draft: false
---

<h1><o:p>&nbsp;</o:p></h1>

<br clear="all" style="page-break-before: always;" />

<h1><a name="_Toc183973552">STRUCTURE OF 2 SAMUEL 7:1-17</a><o:p></o:p></h1>

<h2><a name="_Toc183973553"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">Outline</span></a><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;"><o:p></o:p></span></h2>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l0 level1 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -.25in; text-indent: -.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>I.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>David announces his desire
(1-2)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->a.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>David is settled in his
house (1a)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->b.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>David is rested from his
enemies (1b)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->c.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>David tells Nathan his
desire (2)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l0 level1 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -.25in; text-indent: -.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>II.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Nathan responds but is
redirected (3-4)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->a.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Nathan approves (3)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->b.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh redirects Nathan’s
approval (4)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l0 level1 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -.25in; text-indent: -.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp; </span>III.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh’s Subversive
Reversal (5-16)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->a.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh questions David’s
desire to subvert it (5)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->b.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh considers Isreal’s
history (6-10)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>i.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh speaks to Isreal’s
history (6-7)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>ii.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh took David from
obscurity (8)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>iii.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh made David leader over
Isreal (9)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>iv.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh made a “place” for Israel
(10)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.0in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level2 lfo1; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]-->c.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh subverts David’s
request establishing The Covenant (11-16)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>i.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh will build David a
“house” (11)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>ii.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh promises David a
dynasty (12)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>iii.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh confirms David’s
kingdom in his offspring (13)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>iv.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh adopts the offspring
(14a)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>v.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh warns of the
offspring’s correction (14b)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>vi.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh promises love to the
offspring, unlike Saul (15)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; margin-left: 1.5in; mso-add-space: auto; mso-list: l0 level3 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -9.0pt; text-indent: -1.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span>vii.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Yahweh confirms David’s
dynasty forever (16)<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l0 level1 lfo1; mso-text-indent-alt: -.25in; text-indent: -.5in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp; </span>IV.<span style="font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Nathan tells the vision to
David (17)<o:p></o:p></p>

<h2><a name="_Toc183973554"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">Structural Analysis</span></a><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;"><o:p></o:p></span></h2>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">The pericope in 2
Samuel 7:1-17<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn1" name="_ftnref1" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[1]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
is often referred to as The Davidic Covenant. While the passage does not use
the term “covenant” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">בְּרִית</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>, <i>b’riyt</i>)<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn2" name="_ftnref2" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[2]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>,
Grisanti acknowledged that other passages “make clear that it provides the
initial delineation of the Davidic Covenant (Pss 89:3-4).”<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn3" name="_ftnref3" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[3]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
Williamson suggests that the promise of a “great name” is indication of a
covenant promise by Yahweh.<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn4" name="_ftnref4" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[4]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;"><i>David announces
his desire (1-2) -</i> After having settled in his new tree-built “house”
(<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">בַּיִת</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>, <i>bayit</i>) and having brought the Ark
of Yahweh into the new capital city, David acknowledges his cognitive
dissonance. Essentially David says, “How can I get this big house, when Yahweh
is in a tent?” In Ancient Near East (ANE) cultures, “it is at the outset of his
reign that a king ought to show the piety of seeking divine permission for the
construction of a temple.”<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn5" name="_ftnref5" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[5]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
This permission implies that the “divine rest then often results in rest for
the people in their land” and that it was a “common occurrence” for “a
victorious king to show his gratitude to the patron deity.”<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn6" name="_ftnref6" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[6]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;"><i>Nathan responds
but is redirected (3-4) - </i>Initially, Nathan sees this as acceptable and
reasonable therefore approves of the plan, but Yahweh redirects Nathan in a
night-vision.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;"><i>Yahweh
questions David’s desire, subverting it (5) - </i>Yahweh opens this vision with
a rhetorical question, “Are you the one to build for me a house for my
dwelling?” (5). Which then sets up a series of statements in which Yahweh will
subvert David’s desire and request. This begins the lionshare of the section,
where Yahweh subverts cultural expectations and reverses David’s request. Instead
of the king getting permission for the deity to build a house for the deity,
the deity will build a “house” or “dynasty” for the king. In this instance, the
deity rejects (or at least delays) the offer and reverses the offer. The rest
of the speech is the substance of that subversive reversal.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;"><i>Yahweh
considers Isreal’s history (6-10) - </i>Yahweh begins by reviewing Yisrael’s
history from the times of Moses through the Judges, which establishes a
demarcation into the era of the Monarchy (6-7). He points out that if he wanted
a house, he could have asked for one, but he was quite content with the
tabernacle/tent. Brueggemann sees this as Yahweh finding a “permanent residence”
as “unacceptable because it violates Yahweh’s freedom.”<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn7" name="_ftnref7" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[7]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a> Given
that the pericope ends with Yahweh promising David’s son the opportunity to
build Yahweh a house, this is unlikely the intended understanding. More likely,
Yahweh wishes to drive home the point that David’s request is unnecessary, and
that Yahweh can handle his own affairs. Yahweh transitions from era of
Yisrael’s ancient history to its recent history. He transitioned David from the
role of an obscure shepherd boy to the position of leader of a nation (8-9). This
was not for David’s sake, but for Yisrael’s sake, and by extension Yahweh’s
sake. In discussing the way he used David in this, he brings forward several
word plays which we will discuss in the language section below. Yahweh ends
this section by showing how he used David to “make a place for my people
Yisrael” (10).<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;"><i>Yahweh subverts
David’s request establishing The Covenant (11-17) - </i>Having established
Yahweh’s historical role with Yisrael, the transition into the era of the
monarchy, and David’s role in that transition, Yahweh now subverts David’s
request. Instead of David building a “house” (temple) for Yahweh to match his
“house” (palace), Yahweh will build David a “house” (dynasty) (11-12). The
“rest” promised David in 2 Sam 7:11 may be considered a “deliberate echo”<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn8" name="_ftnref8" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[8]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
of Deut 12:10-11 and will come in the same manner as the Judges of previous
generations (2 Sam 7:7, 1 Chr 17:6).<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn9" name="_ftnref9" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[9]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
Not only will David’s kingship be like the Judges of old (11) and he
will receive a multi-generation kingship (dynasty), but he is promised an
offspring who will get the opportunity David wanted, to build Yahweh a “house”
(temple). As if that promise wasn’t beyond the scale of David’s imagination,
Yahweh ups the ante by adopting David’s offspring as a divine son (a common
trope in the ancient near east, where the king would be considered an adopted
son of the deity) (14a).<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn10" name="_ftnref10" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[10]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
However, at this point Yahweh issues a warning that while this covenant promise
is eternal and ageless, the offspring will be disciplined when they fail (14b).
<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">George
notes that 2 Samuel 7 in its final form is edited and preserved in the Tanakh
because of the larger context of the Babylonian exile to address an existential
crisis; with the destruction of the Temple and conquering of the king, “were
the Israelites of Judeans a distinct people any longer?”<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn11" name="_ftnref11" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[11]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
Yahweh promises love to the offspring, unlike Saul (15) and confirms
David’s dynasty forever (16). This sets the stage for the age of the kings to
come, their frequent failures, and the prophetic hope that a “son of David”
will come. The pericope ends with Nathan telling the vision to David (17), and
Daniel responding in gratitude (18-29).<o:p></o:p></p>

<h1><a name="_Toc183973555">LANGUAGE</a><o:p></o:p></h1>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">The author(s) of 2
Samuel 7 uses conjugated forms of the word “house” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">בַּיִת</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>, <i>bayit</i> - <i>house, dwelling, habitation</i>)<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn12" name="_ftnref12" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[12]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a> with
significant repetition, using nuanced meaning of the word throughout, while
also supplying parallel words to flesh out the concepts. A comparison of the
translation choices<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn13" name="_ftnref13" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[13]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a> shows
where the dynamic range of the word is brought forward by some translators,
which simultaneously obscures the English reader from seeing the interplay of
meaning in the single word “house”. The pericope opens with a pairing of two
words “house” and “tent”. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">In
7:1-2, David’s physical “house” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">בַּיִת</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>,
<i>bayit</i>), made of tree-wood is contrasted with the Ark of God being in a
“tent” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">יְרִיעָה</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>,<i> y’riyah - curtain,
tent by implication</i>).<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn14" name="_ftnref14" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[14]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
David observes that he has finally settled as king in a nice house (a king’s
house, by inference, is a palace, which is choice made by CEB and NET), but the
Ark of God (God’s Presence) is in a tent (the wilderness tabernacle). <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">In
7:5-7, Yahweh pushes back on David’s desire with a counter perspective. Yahweh
reminds David that the “tent” was Yahweh’s house since the times of Moses, and
he never asked the people to build him a house. The CEB uses “Temple” in these
verses, which is conceptually what will be built and will be the focus of the
biblical authors afterward. However, the interplay between David’s “house” and
Yahweh’s “house” should not be lost. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">In
7:8, The author makes another word play here, subverting the idea of “house”. Yahweh
took David from the “pasture” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">נָוֶה</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>,<i> naveh</i>) which
is translated pasture, but literally is the “abode/house of the shepherd/flock”
and “from behind” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">מֵאַחַ֖ר</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>, <i>me'achar</i>) the
sheep to become a leader, literally “one in front” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">נָגִיד</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>,
<i>nagiyd</i>)<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn15" name="_ftnref15" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[15]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
“over the people” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">עַל־עַמִּ֖י</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>, <i>al-amiy</i>). So
then, putting this in a wooden English for the sake of making it clear: “I
(Yahweh) took you (David) from the abode/house of the Shepherd and from behind
the sheep to become one in front of and over The People, over Yisrael.”<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn16" name="_ftnref16" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[16]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">In
7:9-10, Yahweh carries forward the poetic references between David’s
agricultural past and his kingly future, by saying he will “make a place” for
Israel, and he will “plant” them so they can “dwell” in their “place”. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">In
7:11, Yahweh pivots from the present circumstance (David’s secure kingship)
into a forward looking at David’s dynastic future. Yahweh declares that Yahweh
will build a “house” for David. In this instance and through the end of the
speech, Yahweh uses the word house to mean dynasty. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">In
7:12-15, Yahweh turns the focus to David’s offspring, who will be established
on David’s throne forever, and Yahweh makes covenant promises to David’s
offspring, he uses the word “house” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">בַּיִת</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>,
bayit) but this time with the nuance of household or dynasty, rather than
physical building.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">In
7:16, Yahweh says David’s “house”, “kingdom”, and “throne” shall endure
“forever” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">עַד־עוֹלָֽם</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>, <i>ad-olam, lasting
time-long time</i>). This phrase does not always mean “forever”, it can mean
“for a limited period of time” or “for a very long time”.<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn17" name="_ftnref17" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[17]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
When “olam” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">עוֹלָם</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>, ʿôlām, “long time”)
is combined with “ad” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">עַד</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>,<i>ʿad</i>, “lasting time”) it is often translated as “forever and ever”.<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn18" name="_ftnref18" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[18]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
The context and emphasis of the section emphasis the on-going nature of the
Davidic Promise, as though something is riding on this for Yahweh that goes
beyond David himself.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">In
7:17, closes Yahweh’s speech and transitions from Nathan telling David about
Yahweh’s reply to David’s response (18-29).<o:p></o:p></p>

<h1><a name="_Toc183973556">LITERARY CONTEXT</a><o:p></o:p></h1>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; The
books of 1 Samuel and 2 Samuel are considered one work in two parts and may not
have been split into two until the Greek translation of Tanakh into the LXX.<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn19" name="_ftnref19" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[19]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
The book of 1 Samuel establishes Samuel as the prophet and last of the Judges,
Yisrael’s rejection of Yahweh and request for a king, and the rise and fall of
Saul as king. This first book also introduces Saul’s replacement in David. The
book of 2 Samuel picks up at Saul’s death and the rise, fall, and redemption of
David.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; We find the David Covenant in the first portion of 2
Samuel, followed by the various exploits and failures of David in his role as
King. We see the importance of the king’s relationship with Yahweh, and the
relevance of repentance and humility in failure. Burleson notes that this
section comes on the heels of chapter 5 where David “became greater and
greater” and that he had been established (2 Sam 5:4-5, 10-12; 1 Chron
12:23-40).<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn20" name="_ftnref20" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[20]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>2 Samuel 1-6 establish
David as king and end with the ark being brought into Yerushalayim but end with
a foreshadowing of David’s chief failure (women). <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-weight: bold; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span><b>2 Samuel 7 is the
establishment of the Davidic Dynasty and the promise of a never-ending kingdom.
<o:p></o:p></b></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>2 Samuel 8-10 show
David securing The Land for Yisrael.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>2 Samuel 11-12 shows
David’s failure, confrontation, and how he responds more humbly than Saul when
he failed.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>2 Samuel 13-21 show the
hardship brought about by David’s failures, and Saul’s.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>2 Samuel 22-23 show David’s
gratitude for Yahweh’s interventions.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast" style="line-height: 115%; mso-list: l1 level1 lfo2; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>2 Samuel 24 shows a
final failure of David to trust Yahweh, again shows he responds more faithfully
even in failure than Saul, and his repentance marks the future site for the
temple.<o:p></o:p></p>

<h1><a name="_Toc183973557">INTERTEXTUAL CONNECTIONS – PSALM 89</a><o:p></o:p></h1>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">Psalm
89 is one of the key Divine Council sections in the Tanakh,<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn21" name="_ftnref21" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[21]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
and was attributed to Ethan the Ezrahite.<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn22" name="_ftnref22" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[22]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
The psalm begins drawing on the Davidic Covenant (2 Sam 7) and the on-going
nature of the promise “from generation to generation” (Ps 89:4). The author
then transitions to Yahweh’s dominance over the gods and his incomparability (6-8),
and Yahweh’s dominance over all kingdoms and nature itself (9-16). The author
speaks of Yahweh as Yisrael’s “shield” (17-18) and then transitions back to the
Davidic Promise that an offspring of David would always it on the throne (19-37).
<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">It
is in verse 38 where we discover the reason for all this emphasis on Yahweh’s
promise and greatness and power, because “you have spurned and rejected” and
“repudiated the covenant” and “defiled his crown” and “broken down his walls”
and “made his enemies rejoice” and “cast his throne to the ground” (38-45). The
author is struggling with the cognitive dissonance of Yahweh’s greatness and
undying loyalty and his promise to David, against the current circumstances of exiled
peoples of Yisrael. This makes it clear that this Psalm is being written in
exile looking to this Davidic Covenant in 2 Sam 7 for hope.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">Now
we get to the real question burning in the heart of the author, “How long, O
Yahweh? Will you hide yourself forever” (46). He pleads with Yahweh to remember
his covenant and restore David’s throne. He makes a statement here that begins
to pull on Messianic hopes for Yeshua followers, “What man can live and not see
death?” (48). This is a reference to Sheol, but later Yeshua followers,
post-resurrection, will see this as a foreshadowing. He ends with a grasping
hope in Yahweh despite their present circumstances (52).<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%; text-indent: .5in;">Throughout
2 Samuel 7, “house” is used to refer to David’s physical home, Yahweh’s future
home, and David’s dynasty. The “house of the LORD” becomes a common reference
to the later temple worship (1 Kgs 6:1; 2 Chr 3:3; Isa 56:7; Ps 122:1; Jer
7:2). The prophets look to this Davidic promise hoping for a “root of Jesse”
and David’s “righteous branch” (Isa 11:1; 11:10; Jer 23:5; 33:15). The Psalmist
is calling for Yahweh to fulfill his promise to keep an offspring of David on
the throne forever. Many centuries later, Rabbi Yeshua (Jesus) is referred to
as the Son of David (Matt 1:1; 9:27; 15:22; 20:30-31; 21:9; 22:42; Mark
10:47-48). Rabbi Yeshua (Jesus) referred to the temple as his Father’s <b><i>house</i></b>
(Luke 2:49; John 2:16).<o:p></o:p></p>

<h1><a name="_Toc183973558">HERMENEUTICS - THEOLOGICAL THEMES &amp; WISDOM FOR
TODAY</a><o:p></o:p></h1>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; The
Way of Yahweh is that of subversive reversal. In 2 Samuel 7, we see David
following his cultural expectation to build a “house” for his patron deity.
Yahweh subverts that expectation. The life of Rabbi Yeshua was largely a
subversion and reversal of the mixed and muddy Messianic expectations of Second
Temple Judaism. As we navigate The Way in the modern era, we may do well to be
aware of moments in which Yahweh will subvert and reverse a cultural
expectation of ours. Gilliard notes that the early Yeshua followers found a
systemic injustice in their community (unfair distribution to widows of
different groups), and the subversive nature of the kingdom caused them to
address this injustice.<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn23" name="_ftnref23" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[23]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a> When
we see voices from inside the dominant culture taking aim at the most
vulnerable in society, we can subvert expectations by being an advocate for
those groups. Instead of hating our enemy, we can subvert the expectations and
love them well instead.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; By
comparing the Davidic Covenant (a promise of blessing and punishment) to the
downline exile experience of the author of Psalm 89, we can see what it feels
like to suffer cognitive dissonance between what one believes and what one is
experiencing in real current space-time.&nbsp;
We can all find moments where we can point to “God is good” and find we
did not experience that in a specific instance. In Psalm 89, like the book of Job,
we wrestle with the idealist of Proverbs “God blesses his faithful” which is
offset by the pessimism of Ecclesiastes “… but sometimes He doesn’t.” We have
experiences in life where we “prayed and agreed as touching a matter” and it
didn’t come to pass. In those moments, we find ourselves wondering if God is faithful
or if his word is true. Along with the Psalmist, we can cite promises and
stories, while lamenting our current outcomes. We can take ownership of our
part, including our failures or the failures of our community, while hoping for
better.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; The
Davidic Covenant of 2 Samuel 7:1-17 is a set of promises, but those promises are
within a larger context. Often, we can get bogged down in a hyper-focus on one
aspect of living with Yahweh as our patron deity, but when we take a step back
to the larger vision, we can get a deeper perspective. Psalm 89 reminds us that
Yahweh is working with rebel elohim, rebel human kingdoms, and rebel
Yahwehists, and balancing those in view of the long-term eschatological
kingdom. With our hindsight in the 21<sup>st</sup> century, we see that the Son
of David came over 2,000 years ago, yet he has not returned in his final glory as
he promised. As the psalmist lamented the current state of The People in light
of the Davidic hope, we lament the final filling-full of the Davidic Hope. We
still await the Son of David, the root of Jesse’, to sit upon his throne in
Yerushalayim, where the nations of earth will serve and obey only him, King of
kings, and God of gods. We still look forward to a day when Death is defeated
and Sheol is empty. We live in the now and not yet Kingdom of God. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Nathan
did not miss the mark by agreeing to David’s request too soon, but he may have
done well to wait. Could we, too, pause and seek Yahweh’s guidance before we
proceed with what we <i>think</i> his will might be in any given situation? <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpFirst" style="line-height: 200%; mso-list: l2 level1 lfo3; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Like David, we can look for
subversion in the subtlies of Yahweh. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpMiddle" style="line-height: 200%; mso-list: l2 level1 lfo3; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Like Nathan, we can pause
and seek before speaking too quickly. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoListParagraphCxSpLast" style="line-height: 200%; mso-list: l2 level1 lfo3; text-indent: -.25in;"><!--[if !supportLists]--><span style="font-family: Symbol; mso-bidi-font-family: Symbol; mso-fareast-font-family: Symbol;">·<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; font-feature-settings: normal; font-kerning: auto; font-optical-sizing: auto; font-size-adjust: none; font-size: 7pt; font-stretch: normal; font-variant-alternates: normal; font-variant-east-asian: normal; font-variant-emoji: normal; font-variant-numeric: normal; font-variant-position: normal; font-variation-settings: normal; line-height: normal;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;
</span></span><!--[endif]--><span dir="LTR"></span>Like the Psalmist, we can look
to the promises even when our present circumstances fail us. <o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; Our
work today is to live the subversive reversal kingdom ways in front of our
neighbor and enemy alike. We live in exile in our own land. We serve empires
and kingdoms while remaining loyal to Yahweh (Yeshua). Rabbi Yeshua was the
example of living the subversive ways of Yahweh before our fellow human imagers.<o:p></o:p></p>

<span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;"><br clear="all" style="mso-special-character: line-break; page-break-before: always;" />
</span>

<p class="MsoNormal"><a name="_APPENDIX_–_COMPARING"></a><o:p>&nbsp;</o:p></p>

<h1><a name="_Toc183973559">APPENDIX – COMPARING “HOUSE” IN TRANSLATIONS</a><o:p></o:p></h1>

<p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 115%;">&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp;&nbsp; In
this chart, the translation choices of several Bible editions have been
compared for the English choice for “house” (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">בַּיִת</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>,
<i>bayit</i>). Each instance where “house” is not used is <b>bolded</b> for
emphasis.<o:p></o:p></p>

<table border="1" cellpadding="0" class="MsoNormalTable" style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; mso-border-insideh: .5pt solid windowtext; mso-border-insidev: .5pt solid windowtext; mso-cellspacing: 1.5pt; mso-yfti-tbllook: 1184; width: 100%;">
 <tbody><tr style="height: 17.0pt; mso-yfti-firstrow: yes; mso-yfti-irow: 0;">
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 17.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 24.72%;" width="24%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">2 Sam 7: Verse<o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 17.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 13.88%;" valign="top" width="13%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">CEB<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn24" name="_ftnref24" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[24]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 17.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 10.8%;" width="10%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">LEB<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn25" name="_ftnref25" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[25]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 17.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 25.16%;" width="25%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">NET<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn26" name="_ftnref26" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[26]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 17.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 10.8%;" width="10%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">ESV<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn27" name="_ftnref27" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[27]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 17.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 10.58%;" width="10%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">KVJ<a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftn28" name="_ftnref28" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 12.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[28]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a><o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr style="height: 31.0pt; mso-yfti-irow: 1;">
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 31.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 24.72%;" width="24%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">1<o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 31.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 13.88%;" width="13%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;"><b>palace<o:p></o:p></b></p>
  </td>
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 31.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 10.8%;" width="10%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">house<o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 31.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 25.16%;" width="25%">

  </td>

  </td>

  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">house<o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
 </tr>
 <tr style="height: 31.0pt; mso-yfti-irow: 2;">
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 31.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 24.72%;" width="24%">
  <p class="MsoNormal" style="line-height: 200%;">2<o:p></o:p></p>
  </td>
  <td style="border: solid windowtext 1.0pt; height: 31.0pt; mso-border-alt: solid windowtext .5pt; padding: .75pt .75pt .75pt .75pt; width: 13.88%;" width="13%">

 </tr>
 <tr style="height: 31.0pt; mso-yfti-irow: 3;">

  </td>

 <tr style="height: 31.0pt; mso-yfti-irow: 5;">
