<p class="MsoNormal"><o:p>&nbsp;</o:p></p>

<br clear="all" style="mso-special-character: line-break; page-break-before: always;" />

<p class="MsoNormal"><o:p>&nbsp;</o:p></p>

<h1><a name="_Toc183973560">BIBLIOGRAPHY</a><o:p></o:p></h1>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_BIBL
{&quot;uncited&quot;:[],&quot;omitted&quot;:[],&quot;custom&quot;:[]}
CSL_BIBLIOGRAPHY <span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Ackroyd, Peter A. <i>The Second Book of
Samuel</i>. The Cambridge Bible Commentary. Cambridge University Press, 1977.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Brueggemann,
Walter. <i>First and Second Samuel</i>. Interpretation: A Bible Commentary for
Teaching and Preaching. Westminster John Knox Press, 1990.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Burleson,
Doug. “God’s Heart Establishes (2 Sam. 7:12, 16)” (n.d.).
https://www.academia.edu/15408955/Gods_Heart_Establishes_2_Sam_7_12_16_.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Douglas
Mangum, Derek R Brown, Rachel Klippenstein, and Rebekah Hurst, eds. <i>The
Lexham Theological Wordbook</i>. Bellingham, WA: Lexham Press, 2014.
https://ref.ly/logosres/lxtheowrdbk?art=art336.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">George,
Mark K. “Fluid Stability in Second Samuel 7.” <i>Cathol. Biblic. Q.</i> 64
(2002): 17–36.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Gesenius,
Wilhelm, Charles Briggs, S.R. Driver, and Francis Brown. <i>The Abridged
Brown-Driver-Briggs Hebrew-English Lexicon of the Old Testament: From A Hebrew
and English Lexicon of the Old Testament.</i> Boston; New York: Houghton,
Mifflin and Company, 1906.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Gilliard,
Dominique DuBois, and Mark Labberton. <i>Subversive Witness: Scripture’s Call
to Leverage Privilege</i>. Grand Rapids, UNITED STATES: Zondervan, 2021.
http://ebookcentral.proquest.com/lib/fuller/detail.action?docID=6666663.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Grisanti,
Michael A. “The Davidic Covenant.” <i>Masters Semin. J.</i> 10.2 (1999):
233–50.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Halpern,
Baruch. <i>David’s Secret Demons: Messiah, Murderer, Traitor, King</i>. The
Bible in Its World. Eerdmans, 2001.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Heiser,
Michael. <i>The Unseen Realm: Recovering the Supernatural Worldview of the
Bible</i>. First edition. Bellingham, WA: Lexham Press, 2015.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">John
Davis, T. Desmond Alexander, and David W. Baker. <i>Dictionary of the Old
Testament: Pentateuch (The IVP Bible Dictionary Series)</i>. The IVP Bible
Dictionary Series. Downers Grove: IVP, 2015.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Mounce,
William D., ed. <i>Mounce’s Complete Expository Dictionary of Old &amp; New
Testament Words</i>. Grand Rapids, Mich: Zondervan, 2006.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Rydelnik,
Michael. <i>The Moody Handbook of Messianic Prophecy: Studies and Expositions
of the Messiah in the Hebrew Bible</i>. Chicago: Moody Publishers, 2019.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Walton,
John H., Victor Harold Matthews, and Mark W. Chavalas. <i>The IVP Bible
Background Commentary: Old Testament</i>. Downers Grove, Ill: InterVarsity
Press, 2000.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography">Wolfe,
Darrell. “Author’s Note:,” n.d.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><i>Common
English Bible: A Fresh Translation to Touch the Heart and Mind</i>. Nashville, TN: Common English Bible,
2011.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><i>Faithlife
Study Bible, via Logos Software</i>. Faithlife / Logos Bible Software, 2018.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><i>New
English Translation (The NET Bible)</i>. Second Edition, Full Notes. Denmark: Biblical Studies
Press, Thomas Nelson, 2019.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><i>The
Holy Bible: English Standard Version (ESV)</i>. Wheaton, Illinois: Crossway Bibles, 2016.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><i>The
Holy Bible: King James Version</i>. Electronic Edition of the 1900 Authorized Version.
Bellingham, WA: Logos Research Systems, Inc., 2009.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><i>The
Lexham English Bible (LEB), Fourth Edition</i>. Logo Bible Software. Harris, W. H., III, Ritzema, E.,
Brannan, R., Mangum, D., Dunham, J., Reimer, J. A., &amp; Wierenga, M. (Eds.).
Bellingham, WA: Lexham Press, 2010. http://www.lexhampress.com.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoBibliography"><i>The
NET Bible First Edition Notes</i>. Biblical Studies Press, 2006.<o:p></o:p></p>

<p class="MsoNormal"><!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p>&nbsp;</o:p></p>

<div><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><br clear="all" />

<hr align="left" size="1" width="33%" />

<!--[endif]-->

<div id="ftn1">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref1" name="_ftn1" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[1]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>

WA&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Logo Bible
Software&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;The Lexham English Bible (LEB), Fourth

Press&quot;}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;All quotes either LEB or my own
paraphrase&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;note&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}

Edition</i>,
Logo Bible Software., Harris, W. H., III, Ritzema, E., Brannan, R., Mangum, D.,
Dunham, J., Reimer, J. A., &amp; Wierenga, M. (Eds.) (Bellingham, WA: Lexham
Press, 2010), n. All quotes either LEB or my own paraphrase,
http://www.lexhampress.com.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn2">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref2" name="_ftn2" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[2]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;4pdRDHsV&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;William
D. Mounce, ed., {\\i{}Mounce\\uc0\\u8217{}s Complete Expository Dictionary of
Old &amp; New Testament Words} (Grand Rapids, Mich: Zondervan, 2006),
\\uc0\\u167{}[1382]
\\uc0\\u1489{}\\uc0\\u1456{}\\uc0\\u1468{}\\uc0\\u1512{}\\uc0\\u1460{}\\uc0\\u1497{}\\uc0\\u1514{}
ber\\uc0\\u238{}t.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;William D. Mounce,
ed., Mounce’s Complete Expository Dictionary of Old &amp; New Testament Words
(Grand Rapids, Mich: Zondervan, 2006), §[1382] <span lang=HE dir=RTL>בְּרִית</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span>
berît.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:2},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:276,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/WGGI4T74&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:276,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;BS440
.M63 2006&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Grand Rapids,
Mich&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-0-310-24878-1&quot;,&quot;language&quot;:&quot;eng
grc heb&quot;,&quot;note&quot;:&quot;OCLC:
ocm64453290&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;1316&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Zondervan&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Grand
Rapids, Mich&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Library of Congress
ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Mounce's complete expository dictionary of
Old &amp; New Testament
words&quot;,&quot;editor&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Mounce&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;William
D.&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2006&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;[1382]
<span lang=HE dir=RTL>בְּרִית</span><span dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span><span

berît&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;section&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}

Complete Expository Dictionary of Old &amp; New Testament Words</i> (Grand
Rapids, Mich: Zondervan, 2006), §[1382] <span dir="RTL" lang="HE">בְּרִית</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>
berît.<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn3">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref3" name="_ftn3" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[3]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;sLTKFWfZ&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Michael
A. Grisanti, \\uc0\\u8220{}The Davidic Covenant,\\uc0\\u8221{} {\\i{}Masters
Semin. J.} 10.2 (1999): 236.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Michael A.
Grisanti, “The Davidic Covenant,” Masters Semin. J. 10.2 (1999):
236.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:3},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2315,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/VJTHIR74&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2315,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;article-journal&quot;,&quot;container-title&quot;:&quot;The
Master's Seminary Journal&quot;,&quot;issue&quot;:&quot;2&quot;,&quot;page&quot;:&quot;233-250&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;The
Davidic
Covenant&quot;,&quot;volume&quot;:&quot;10&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Grisanti&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Michael
A.&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;1999&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;236&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;page&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Michael A. Grisanti, “The Davidic
Covenant,” <i>Masters Semin. J.</i> 10.2 (1999): 236.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn4">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref4" name="_ftn4" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[4]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;Y2L8OdZV&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;John
Davis, T. Desmond Alexander, and David W. Baker, {\\i{}Dictionary of the Old
Testament: Pentateuch (The IVP Bible Dictionary Series)}, The IVP Bible
Dictionary Series (Downers Grove: IVP, 2015), n. p 145 P. R. Williamson,
\\uc0\\u8220{}Covenant,\\uc0\\u8221{}: \\uc0\\u8220{}The prospect of a
\\uc0\\u8220{}great name\\uc0\\u8221{} likewise relates to the overarching
promise of nationhood. This is suggested not only by the implicit contrast with
the failed aspirations of the tower-builders of Babel (Gen 11:4), whose
attempts at civil organization (nationhood) had been thwarted by divine
judgment, but also by the fact that this same language is used in relation to
David (2 Sam 7:9) in the context of national security and international
prestige.\\uc0\\u8221{}.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;John Davis, T.
Desmond Alexander, and David W. Baker, Dictionary of the Old Testament:
Pentateuch (The IVP Bible Dictionary Series), The IVP Bible Dictionary Series
(Downers Grove: IVP, 2015), n. p 145 P. R. Williamson, “Covenant,”: “The
prospect of a “great name” likewise relates to the overarching promise of
nationhood. This is suggested not only by the implicit contrast with the failed
aspirations of the tower-builders of Babel (Gen 11:4), whose attempts at civil
organization (nationhood) had been thwarted by divine judgment, but also by the
fact that this same language is used in relation to David (2 Sam 7:9) in the
context of national security and international
prestige.”.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:4},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:1728,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/3HFBLCIE&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:1728,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;collection-title&quot;:&quot;The
IVP Bible Dictionary Series&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Downers
Grove&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;IVP&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Downers
Grove&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Dictionary of the Old Testament: Pentateuch
(The IVP Bible Dictionary
Series)&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;literal&quot;:&quot;John
Davis&quot;},{&quot;literal&quot;:&quot;T. Desmond
Alexander&quot;},{&quot;literal&quot;:&quot;David W. Baker&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2015&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;p
145 P. R. Williamson, “Covenant,”: “The prospect of a “great name” likewise
relates to the overarching promise of nationhood. This is suggested not only by
the implicit contrast with the failed aspirations of the tower-builders of
Babel (Gen 11:4), whose attempts at civil organization (nationhood) had been
thwarted by divine judgment, but also by the fact that this same language is
used in relation to David (2 Sam 7:9) in the context of national security and
international prestige.”&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;note&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->John Davis, T. Desmond Alexander, and
David W. Baker, <i>Dictionary of the Old Testament: Pentateuch (The IVP Bible
Dictionary Series)</i>, The IVP Bible Dictionary Series (Downers Grove: IVP,
2015), n. p 145 P. R. Williamson, “Covenant,”: “The prospect of a “great name”
likewise relates to the overarching promise of nationhood. This is suggested
not only by the implicit contrast with the failed aspirations of the
tower-builders of Babel (Gen 11:4), whose attempts at civil organization
(nationhood) had been thwarted by divine judgment, but also by the fact that
this same language is used in relation to David (2 Sam 7:9) in the context of
national security and international prestige.”.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn5">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref5" name="_ftn5" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[5]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;0aOCmhZm&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Baruch
Halpern, {\\i{}David\\uc0\\u8217{}s Secret Demons: Messiah, Murderer, Traitor,
King}, The Bible in Its World (Eerdmans, 2001),
337.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Baruch Halpern, David’s Secret
Demons: Messiah, Murderer, Traitor, King, The Bible in Its World (Eerdmans,
2001), 337.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:5},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2316,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/7XT62FP2&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2316,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;collection-title&quot;:&quot;The
Bible in Its
World&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;333-341&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Eerdmans&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;David's
Secret Demons: Messiah, Murderer, Traitor, King&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Halpern&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Baruch&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2001&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;337&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;page&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Baruch Halpern, <i>David’s Secret
Demons: Messiah, Murderer, Traitor, King</i>, The Bible in Its World (Eerdmans,
2001), 337.<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn6">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref6" name="_ftn6" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[6]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;qJpXmste&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;John
H. Walton, Victor Harold Matthews, and Mark W. Chavalas, {\\i{}The IVP Bible
Background Commentary: Old Testament} (Downers Grove, Ill: InterVarsity Press,
2000), n. Notes 7:1-29 A Covenant and a Dynasty for
David.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;John H. Walton, Victor Harold
Matthews, and Mark W. Chavalas, The IVP Bible Background Commentary: Old
Testament (Downers Grove, Ill: InterVarsity Press, 2000), n. Notes 7:1-29 A Covenant
and a Dynasty for
David.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:6},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:1866,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/5GTQXVID&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:1866,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;BS1151.2
.W35 2000&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Downers Grove,
Ill&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-0-8308-1419-0&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;832&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;InterVarsity
Press&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Downers Grove,
Ill&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Library of Congress
ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;The IVP Bible background commentary: Old
Testament&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;The IVP Bible background
commentary&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Walton&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;John
H.&quot;},{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Matthews&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Victor
Harold&quot;},{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Chavalas&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Mark
W.&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2000&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;Notes
7:1-29 A Covenant and a Dynasty for
David&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;note&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->John H. Walton, Victor Harold Matthews,
and Mark W. Chavalas, <i>The IVP Bible Background Commentary: Old Testament</i>
(Downers Grove, Ill: InterVarsity Press, 2000), n. Notes 7:1-29 A Covenant and
a Dynasty for David.<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:
field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn7">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref7" name="_ftn7" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[7]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION

for Teaching and Preaching (Westminster John Knox Press, 1990),

Samuel, Interpretation: A Bible Commentary for Teaching and Preaching
(Westminster John Knox Press, 1990),
253.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:7},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2311,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/9ACTVKDW&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2311,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;collection-title&quot;:&quot;Interpretation:

(Westminster John Knox Press, 1990), 253.<!--[if supportFields]><span

</div>

<div id="ftn8">

<div id="ftn9">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref9" name="_ftn9" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[9]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;XsamWsMR&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;{\\i{}The
NET Bible First Edition Notes} (Biblical Studies Press, 2006), v. 2 Sam 7 Notes
for 7:7 \&quot;9 tn Heb \\uc0\\u8220{}tribes\\uc0\\u8221{} (so KJV, NASB, NCV),
but the parallel passage in 1 Chr 17:6 has
\\uc0\\u8220{}judges.\\uc0\\u8221{}\&quot;.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;The
NET Bible First Edition Notes (Biblical Studies Press, 2006), v. 2 Sam 7 Notes
for 7:7 \&quot;9 tn Heb “tribes” (so KJV, NASB, NCV), but the parallel passage
in 1 Chr 17:6 has
“judges.”\&quot;.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:9},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:44,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/HUU8BEMB&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:44,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Biblical
Studies Press&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;The NET Bible First Edition
Notes&quot;,&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2006&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;2
Sam 7 Notes for 7:7 \&quot;9 tn Heb “tribes” (so KJV, NASB, NCV), but the
parallel passage in 1 Chr 17:6 has
“judges.”\&quot;&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;verse&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]--><i>The NET Bible First Edition Notes</i> (Biblical Studies Press, 2006), v. 2
Sam 7 Notes for 7:7 "9 tn Heb “tribes” (so KJV, NASB, NCV), but the
parallel passage in 1 Chr 17:6 has “judges.”".<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn10">

<div id="ftn11">

</div>

<div id="ftn12">

of the Old Testament: From A Hebrew and English Lexicon of the Old Testament.}
(Boston; New York: Houghton, Mifflin and Company, 1906),
\\uc0\\u1489{}\\uc0\\u1463{}\\uc0\\u1468{}\\uc0\\u1497{}\\uc0\\u1460{}\\uc0\\u1514{}
(bayi).&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Wilhelm Gesenius et al., The

(bayi).&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:12},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:51,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/AK8LUAP8&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:51,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;Whitaker,

Gesenius. Boston; New York: Houghton, Mifflin and
Company.&quot;,&quot;archive&quot;:&quot;Logos Bible

(bayi)&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;page&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}

<div id="ftn14">

(y’riyah)&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;page&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Gesenius et al., <i>Abridged
Brown-Driver-Briggs Hebrew-English Lexicon</i>, <span dir="RTL" lang="HE">יְרִיעָה</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>
(y’riyah).<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn15">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref15" name="_ftn15" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[15]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>

n.m. leader (lit. prob. one in

n.m. leader (lit. prob. one in

Brown-Driver-Briggs Hebrew-English Lexicon</i>, n. <span dir="RTL" lang="HE">נָגִיד</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>
n.m. leader (lit. prob. one in front).<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn16">

<div id="ftn20">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref20" name="_ftn20" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[20]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;KZgWZnO5&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Doug
Burleson, \\uc0\\u8220{}God\\uc0\\u8217{}s Heart Establishes (2 Sam. 7:12,
16)\\uc0\\u8221{} (n.d.),
https://www.academia.edu/15408955/Gods_Heart_Establishes_2_Sam_7_12_16_.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Doug
Burleson, “God’s Heart Establishes (2 Sam. 7:12, 16)” (n.d.),
https://www.academia.edu/15408955/Gods_Heart_Establishes_2_Sam_7_12_16_.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:20},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2411,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/BKQ3XNFT&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2411,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;article-journal&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;As
God through Nathan addressed David in 2 Sam. 7 the divine Father shows why he
is the true and better King. He had already established David as king and now
was prepared to initiate a world-changing promise. As compared to the hearts of
the
mortal&quot;,&quot;language&quot;:&quot;en&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;www.academia.edu&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;God's
Heart Establishes (2 Sam. 7:12, 16)&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;God's
Heart Establishes (2 Sam. 7:12,
16)&quot;,&quot;URL&quot;:&quot;https://www.academia.edu/15408955/Gods_Heart_Establishes_2_Sam_7_12_16_&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Burleson&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Doug&quot;}],&quot;accessed&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2024&quot;,11,17]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}

(2 Sam. 7:12, 16)” (n.d.),
https://www.academia.edu/15408955/Gods_Heart_Establishes_2_Sam_7_12_16_.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn21">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref21" name="_ftn21" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[21]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;PXHQHH1f&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Michael
Heiser, {\\i{}The Unseen Realm: Recovering the Supernatural Worldview of the
Bible}, First edition. (Bellingham, WA: Lexham Press, 2015), 29; \&quot;Psalm
89:5\\uc0\\u8211{}7 (Hebrew: vv. 6\\uc0\\u8211{}8) explicitly contradicts the
notion of a divine council in which the elohim are
humans.\&quot;.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Michael Heiser, The
Unseen Realm: Recovering the Supernatural Worldview of the Bible, First
edition. (Bellingham, WA: Lexham Press, 2015), 29; \&quot;Psalm 89:5–7 (Hebrew:
vv. 6–8) explicitly contradicts the notion of a divine council in which the
elohim are
humans.\&quot;.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:21},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:20,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/7SP8XMJB&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:20,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;BS680.S86
H45 2015&quot;,&quot;edition&quot;:&quot;First edition&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Bellingham,
WA&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-1-57799-556-2&quot;,&quot;note&quot;:&quot;OCLC:
ocn907177749&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;413&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Lexham
Press&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Bellingham,
WA&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Library of Congress
ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;The unseen realm: recovering the
supernatural worldview of the Bible&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;The
unseen realm&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Heiser&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Michael&quot;}],&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2015&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;29;
\&quot;Psalm 89:5–7 (Hebrew: vv. 6–8) explicitly contradicts the notion of a divine
council in which the elohim are
humans.\&quot;&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;page&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Michael Heiser, <i>The Unseen Realm:
Recovering the Supernatural Worldview of the Bible</i>, First edition.
(Bellingham, WA: Lexham Press, 2015), 29; "Psalm 89:5–7 (Hebrew: vv. 6–8)
explicitly contradicts the notion of a divine council in which the elohim are
humans.".<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn22">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref22" name="_ftn22" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[22]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;wOekw763&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Wolfe,
\\uc0\\u8220{}Author\\uc0\\u8217{}s Note:,\\uc0\\u8221{} n.Ethan the Ezrahite
who is among the wise men compared to Solmon (David\\uc0\\u8217{}s offspring
who ended up building the \\uc0\\u8220{}house\\uc0\\u8221{} of Yahweh) (1 Kings
4:31). LBD says that Ezrahite is a likely a title, but the meaning is unknown.<span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>However, both the individuals, Heman (Ps 88)
and Ethan (Ps 89) who were given this title were sons of Zerah (the son of
Tamar) (1 Chr. 2:4-6).<span style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>It is likely that
Ezrahite
(\\uc0\\u1488{}\\uc0\\u1462{}\\uc0\\u1494{}\\uc0\\u1456{}\\uc0\\u1512{}\\uc0\\u1464{}\\uc0\\u1495{}\\uc0\\u1460{}\\uc0\\u1497{},
ez\\uc0\\u8217{}rachi) is a derivative family name for those who came from of
Zerah
(\\uc0\\u1494{}\\uc0\\u1462{}\\uc0\\u1431{}\\uc0\\u1512{}\\uc0\\u1463{}\\uc0\\u1495{},
zerah) for which the ABDB agrees.<span style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>This
would indicate that either these attributions are to later offspring of this
line by the same name, or more likely that they are pseudepigraphal writings
attempting to pull the ancient contexts of Yisrael\\uc0\\u8217{}s history into
the current calamity.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Wolfe, “Author’s
Note:,” n.Ethan the Ezrahite who is among the wise men compared to Solmon
(David’s offspring who ended up building the “house” of Yahweh) (1 Kings 4:31).
LBD says that Ezrahite is a likely a title, but the meaning is unknown.<span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>However, both the individuals, Heman (Ps 88)
and Ethan (Ps 89) who were given this title were sons of Zerah (the son of
Tamar) (1 Chr. 2:4-6).<span style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>It is likely that
Ezrahite (<span lang=HE dir=RTL>אֶזְרָחִי</span><span dir=LTR></span><span
dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span>, ez’rachi) is a
derivative family name for those who came from of Zerah (<span lang=HE dir=RTL>זֶ֗רַח</span><span
dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span>,
zerah) for which the ABDB agrees.<span style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>This
would indicate that either these attributions are to later offspring of this
line by the same name, or more likely that they are pseudepigraphal writings
attempting to pull the ancient contexts of Yisrael’s history into the current
calamity.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:22},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:1050,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/U7KLYHJD&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:1050,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;document&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Author's
Note:&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Wolfe&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Darrell&quot;}]},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;<span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>Ethan the Ezrahite who is among the wise men
compared to Solmon (David’s offspring who ended up building the “house” of
Yahweh) (1 Kings 4:31). LBD says that Ezrahite is a likely a title, but the
meaning is unknown.<span style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>However, both the
individuals, Heman (Ps 88) and Ethan (Ps 89) who were given this title were
sons of Zerah (the son of Tamar) (1 Chr. 2:4-6).<span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>It is likely that Ezrahite (<span lang=HE
dir=RTL>אֶזְרָחִי</span><span dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span><span
dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span>, ez’rachi) is a derivative family name for
those who came from of Zerah (<span lang=HE dir=RTL>זֶ֗רַח</span><span dir=LTR></span><span
dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span><span dir=LTR></span>, zerah) for which the
ABDB agrees.<span style='mso-spacerun:yes'>  </span>This would indicate that
either these attributions are to later offspring of this line by the same name,
or more likely that they are pseudepigraphal writings attempting to pull the
ancient contexts of Yisrael’s history into the current
calamity.&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;note&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Wolfe, “Author’s Note:,” n.Ethan the
Ezrahite who is among the wise men compared to Solmon (David’s offspring who
ended up building the “house” of Yahweh) (1 Kings 4:31). LBD says that Ezrahite
is a likely a title, but the meaning is unknown.&nbsp; However, both the individuals, Heman (Ps 88)
and Ethan (Ps 89) who were given this title were sons of Zerah (the son of
Tamar) (1 Chr. 2:4-6).&nbsp; It is likely that
Ezrahite (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">אֶזְרָחִי</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>, ez’rachi) is a
derivative family name for those who came from of Zerah (<span dir="RTL" lang="HE">זֶ֗רַח</span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span><span dir="LTR"></span>,
zerah) for which the ABDB agrees.&nbsp; This
would indicate that either these attributions are to later offspring of this
line by the same name, or more likely that they are pseudepigraphal writings
attempting to pull the ancient contexts of Yisrael’s history into the current
calamity.<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn23">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref23" name="_ftn23" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[23]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;52yZtFUz&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;Dominique
DuBois Gilliard and Mark Labberton, {\\i{}Subversive Witness: Scripture\\uc0\\u8217{}s
Call to Leverage Privilege} (Grand Rapids, UNITED STATES: Zondervan, 2021),
22\\uc0\\u8211{}23,
http://ebookcentral.proquest.com/lib/fuller/detail.action?docID=6666663.&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Dominique
DuBois Gilliard and Mark Labberton, Subversive Witness: Scripture’s Call to
Leverage Privilege (Grand Rapids, UNITED STATES: Zondervan, 2021), 22–23,
http://ebookcentral.proquest.com/lib/fuller/detail.action?docID=6666663.&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:23},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2413,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/H8JJ8TLF&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2413,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;abstract&quot;:&quot;Learn
to leverage privilege. Privilege is a social consequence of our unwillingness
to reckon with and turn from sin. But properly stewarded, it can help us see
and participate in God's inbreaking kingdom. Scripture repeatedly affirms that
privilege is real and declares that, rather than exploiting it for selfish gain
or feeling immobilized by it, Christians have a responsibility to leverage it.
Subversive Witness asks us to grapple with privilege, indifference, and
systemic sin in new ways by using biblical examples to reveal the complex
nature of privilege and Christians' responsibility in stewarding it well.
Dominique DuBois Gilliard highlights several people in the Bible who understood
this kingdom call. Through their stories, you will discover how to leverage privilege
to: Resist Sin Stand in Solidarity with the Oppressed Birth Liberation Create
Systemic Change Proclaim the Good News Generate Social Transformation By
embodying Scripture's subversive call to leverage--and at times
forsake--privilege, readers will learn to love their neighbors sacrificially,
enact systemic change, and grow more Christlike as citizens of God's
kingdom.&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Grand Rapids, UNITED
STATES&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-0-310-12404-7&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Zondervan&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Grand
Rapids, UNITED STATES&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;ProQuest Ebook
Central&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Subversive Witness: Scripture's Call to
Leverage Privilege&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;Subversive
Witness&quot;,&quot;URL&quot;:&quot;http://ebookcentral.proquest.com/lib/fuller/detail.action?docID=6666663&quot;,&quot;author&quot;:[{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Gilliard&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Dominique
DuBois&quot;},{&quot;family&quot;:&quot;Labberton&quot;,&quot;given&quot;:&quot;Mark&quot;}],&quot;accessed&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2024&quot;,11,17]]},&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2021&quot;]]}},&quot;locator&quot;:&quot;22-23&quot;,&quot;label&quot;:&quot;page&quot;}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]-->Dominique DuBois Gilliard and Mark
Labberton, <i>Subversive Witness: Scripture’s Call to Leverage Privilege</i>
(Grand Rapids, UNITED STATES: Zondervan, 2021), 22–23,
http://ebookcentral.proquest.com/lib/fuller/detail.action?docID=6666663.<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn24">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref24" name="_ftn24" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[24]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;HRi9TpUU&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;{\\i{}Common
English Bible: A Fresh Translation to Touch the Heart and Mind} (Nashville, TN:
Common English Bible, 2011).&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;Common
English Bible: A Fresh Translation to Touch the Heart and Mind (Nashville, TN:
Common English Bible,
2011).&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:24},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2300,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/ZRCZP44D&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2300,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;call-number&quot;:&quot;BS195
.C58 2011d&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Nashville,
TN&quot;,&quot;ISBN&quot;:&quot;978-1-60926-011-8&quot;,&quot;number-of-pages&quot;:&quot;906&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Common
English Bible&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Nashville, TN&quot;,&quot;source&quot;:&quot;Library
of Congress ISBN&quot;,&quot;title&quot;:&quot;Common English Bible: a fresh
translation to touch the heart and
mind&quot;,&quot;title-short&quot;:&quot;Common English Bible
(CEB)&quot;,&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2011&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]--><i>Common English Bible: A Fresh
Translation to Touch the Heart and Mind</i> (Nashville, TN: Common English Bible, 2011).<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn25">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref25" name="_ftn25" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[25]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>

<div id="ftn26">

2019).&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;New English Translation (The NET

<div id="ftn27">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref27" name="_ftn27" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[27]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>

style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;ppmFwcEN&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;{\\i{}The

style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

<div id="ftn28">

<p class="MsoFootnoteText"><a href="https://d.docs.live.net/6a09c1440da84011/_Education/Fuller%20Theological%20Seminary%20(FTS)/Courses/z.Old_Testament_Introduction_OT500/Final%20Paper/FinalPaper_OT500_DarrelLWolfe.docx#_ftnref28" name="_ftn28" title=""><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><!--[if !supportFootnotes]--><span class="MsoFootnoteReference"><span style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;,serif; font-size: 10.0pt; line-height: 107%; mso-ansi-language: EN-US; mso-ascii-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-bidi-language: HE; mso-bidi-theme-font: major-bidi; mso-fareast-font-family: Aptos; mso-fareast-language: EN-US; mso-fareast-theme-font: minor-latin; mso-hansi-theme-font: major-bidi;">[28]</span></span><!--[endif]--></span></a>
<!--[if supportFields]><span style='mso-element:field-begin'></span><span
style='mso-spacerun:yes'> </span>ADDIN ZOTERO_ITEM CSL_CITATION
{&quot;citationID&quot;:&quot;ygzUfvaZ&quot;,&quot;properties&quot;:{&quot;formattedCitation&quot;:&quot;{\\i{}The
Holy Bible: King James Version}, Electronic Edition of the 1900 Authorized
Version. (Bellingham, WA: Logos Research Systems, Inc.,
2009).&quot;,&quot;plainCitation&quot;:&quot;The Holy Bible: King James
Version, Electronic Edition of the 1900 Authorized Version. (Bellingham, WA:
Logos Research Systems, Inc., 2009).&quot;,&quot;noteIndex&quot;:28},&quot;citationItems&quot;:[{&quot;id&quot;:2302,&quot;uris&quot;:[&quot;http://zotero.org/users/6953150/items/53C7LKNB&quot;],&quot;itemData&quot;:{&quot;id&quot;:2302,&quot;type&quot;:&quot;book&quot;,&quot;edition&quot;:&quot;Electronic
Edition of the 1900 Authorized
Version&quot;,&quot;event-place&quot;:&quot;Bellingham,
WA&quot;,&quot;publisher&quot;:&quot;Logos Research Systems, Inc.&quot;,&quot;publisher-place&quot;:&quot;Bellingham,

Version&quot;,&quot;issued&quot;:{&quot;date-parts&quot;:[[&quot;2009&quot;]]}}}],&quot;schema&quot;:&quot;https://github.com/citation-style-language/schema/raw/master/csl-citation.json&quot;}
<span style='mso-element:field-separator'></span><![endif]--><i>The Holy Bible: King James Version</i>, Electronic Edition of the 1900
Authorized Version. (Bellingham, WA: Logos Research Systems, Inc., 2009).<!--[if supportFields]><span
style='mso-element:field-end'></span><![endif]--><o:p></o:p></p>

</div>

</div><br /><br />

<span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;;">Shalom&nbsp;<span face="sans-serif" style="background-color: white; color: #202122; font-size: 14px;">שָׁלוֹם</span>: Live Long and Prosper!</span>
<br />

<div class="MsoNormal"><span style="font-family: &quot;tempus sans itc&quot;; font-size: 18pt;">Darrell Wolfe</span>
</div>
